summary: First-time contributor PR that ultimately landed a partial lbmemory decomp: fn_80015184 was added as an OSAlarm callback-style function that copies memory in capped chunks from a manager at &g_alloc + 0x6A0, asserts on zero size, invokes a completion callback, or reschedules an alarm. The PR also exported the prototype and added address comments to hsd_3B34.h. Reviewer feedback says the hsd_3B34 function work was superseded by higher-scoring work in #2488, but lbmemory could still merge.
what_changed: Added static assert/file strings in lbmemory.c: lbl_803BA2C0 = "lbmemory.c" and lbMemory_804D3794 = "p->size".
Introduced a local struct LBMgr layout containing OSAlarm alarm, src, dst, size, offset, cb_arg, and cb fields.

Added fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context), which reads the LBMgr from ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0), asserts if size is zero, copies min(size - offset, 0x19000) bytes with memcpy, advances offset, calls cb(0, cb_arg, 0, 0) on completion, or calls OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm to continue later.
Updated lbmemory.h to include <dolphin/os/OSAlarm.h> and declare /* 015184 */ void fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context).
Updated hsd_3B34.h prototypes to include project-style address comments such as /* 3B3408 */ hsd_803B3408 and /* 3B6BE4 */ hsd_803B6BE4.
decomp.dev reported fn_80015184 improving from 0.00% to 88.61%, lbmemory .sdata improving from 66.67% to 83.33%, and a small lbmemory .data regression from 93.33% to 92.18%.
smart_moves: Kept the mergeable portion small after overlapping hsd_3B34 work was superseded by #2488, allowing the lbmemory improvement to land.
Recovered an explicit LBMgr field layout at concrete offsets, making the global-offset access through g_alloc easier to reason about without inventing a broader symbol.
Used typed Dolphin OS callback parameters and added the needed OSAlarm include instead of leaving the header with untyped callback dependencies.
Preserved exact static string objects for assert text, which is often necessary for data-section matching in this codebase.
Kept register-influenced temporary names and unsigned constants in the decompiled function, prioritizing match quality over premature cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When a function is an OSAlarm callback, match the signature with OSAlarm* and OSContext* and make the required Dolphin OS header dependency explicit in the module header.
For manager data accessed through a known global offset such as &g_alloc + 0x6A0, a local struct with documented offsets can clarify code while preserving matching constraints.
Exact chunking logic matters: this function uses a 0x19000-byte cap before memcpy and compares updated offset against size to decide completion.
Assert strings may need to be materialized as exact static arrays to improve or preserve .sdata/.data matching.
Coordinate with adjacent PRs targeting the same unmatched unit; reviewer feedback shows hsd_3B34 work can be displaced by a later PR with higher match scores.
naming_conventions: Unidentified or not-yet-semantically-named functions may remain in fn_80015184 address form even inside a module that otherwise has lbMemory_* names.
Existing lbmemory public functions use address-suffixed module names such as lbMemory_800150F0 and lbMemory_8001529C.
Header declarations use leading address comments, e.g. /* 015184 */ for lbmemory and /* 3B3408 */ for hsd_3B34.
Static labels can retain address-derived names such as lbl_803BA2C0 and lbMemory_804D3794 when semantic naming is not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Author explicitly cited using objdiff and python tools/decomp.py to pick and decompile functions.
The function keeps PPC/register-style temporaries temp_r3, temp_r3_2, temp_r6, and var_r30, suggesting match-driven control over evaluation order.
The manager pointer is formed with a byte-pointer cast, ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0), to hit the required address arithmetic.
Unsigned casts and constants such as 0U, 0x127U, 0x19000U, and 0x10624DD3 are preserved to influence generated code.
The memcpy operands use pointer arithmetic on src and dst plus the saved offset, matching the staged copy behavior instead of abstracting it away.
review_feedback: ribbanya approved the PR and wrote: "Thanks for the contribution! Unfortunately the functions from `hsd_3B34` were just submitted with higher match scores in #2488, but we can still merge `lbmemory`."
No inline review comments were present in the slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported 5 unmatched-item improvements, including hsd_3B34 fn_803B376C and fn_803B61B4, but the human review indicates the hsd_3B34 function work was not the reason this PR was merged.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2488 for the hsd_3B34 functions that superseded this PR's attempted hsd work.
Search later history for a semantic rename or cleanup of fn_80015184.
Check whether the local LBMgr layout or the &g_alloc + 0x6A0 access was later promoted into a shared header or named global.
Investigate whether the small lbmemory .data regression reported by decomp.dev was later recovered.
Search for OSSetAlarm calls using 0x10624DD3 to understand this alarm scheduling pattern.
